updated_conclusion: The Js5WorkerThread-labeled provider cluster is now mapped beyond its string label. It has a descriptor row at 0x140B99238, setup routine 0x14058F122-0x14058F508, adjacent worker-name prelude 0x14058F0E0-0x14058F122, and tiny descriptor materializer thunks around 0x1405A4D20/0x1405A4D60 that are not covered by .pdata.

tooling_milestone.why_it_matters: Provider clusters frequently contain no-.pdata thunks beside real runtime-function owners. Native lookup now separates exact function bodies from adjacent thunks without relying on Ghidra-only helper scripts.

references.note: shared worker-name bootstrap also referencing client, MixerWorker, and VorbisWorker
adjacent prelude immediately before the provider setup function

provider_descriptor.materializer_shape: LEA RAX,[0x140B99238]; MOV [RDX],RAX; MOV RAX,[RCX+0x8]; MOV [RDX+0x8],RAX; RET

sibling_descriptor.materializer_shape: LEA RAX,[0x140B99268]; MOV [RDX],RAX; MOV RAX,[RCX+0x8]; MOV [RDX+0x8],RAX; RET

function_lifecycle.evidence: References Js5WorkerThread at 0x14058F0E8 and calls 0x14066DC10 with the name in RDX.

function_lifecycle.role: provider setup and record construction routine
evidence: Initializes a ref-counted object with vtable 0x140B992C8 and passes Js5WorkerThread to 0x14066DC10.
Stores a value into [published_object+0x3D0] after descriptor publication.
Constructs a secondary ref-counted record with vtable 0x140B99190 and writes byte/string-like fields through helper 0x14002BFA0.

function_lifecycle.evidence: Direct call at 0x14059EC18 materializes descriptor 0x140B99268, then prepares stack arguments and calls 0x14059D7B0.

function_lifecycle.role: stream parser/dispatcher family tied to vtable 0x140B99190
function_lifecycle.evidence: Entry consumes bytes from [RDX+0x10]/[RDX+0x18], dispatches through a jump table, and performs endian-aware integer reads using global 0x140B2708C.

function_lifecycle.role: stream serializer family tied to vtable 0x140B99190
function_lifecycle.evidence: Serializes an opcode-like 0x0A byte, endian-aware u16, optional payload bytes, and passes a constructed record to 0x14066CEE0.

runtime_function_observations: 0x14058F0E0 and 0x14058F122 are exact .pdata function starts and are adjacent.
0x1405A4D20 and 0x1405A4D60 are executable thunks between .pdata entries, so exact .pdata lookup correctly reports no containing runtime function.
0x14059EC18 is inside 0x14059E45B-0x14059F0E5, giving a concrete owner for the direct call to the sibling materializer.
0x14059F140 and 0x140591F20 are exact .pdata starts for serializer/parser entry stubs.

bounded_inference.statement: This cluster is most likely a Js5 worker/thread provider registration path plus adjacent stream/record serializer plumbing. It bridges from worker-name bootstrap into provider descriptor dispatch, but it is not yet proven to be the JS5 cache protocol constructor or key consumer.
bounded_inference.confidence: high for addresses, references, descriptor materializer shapes, and runtime-function ownership; medium for the stream/record serializer role; low-medium for protocol-specific naming beyond the Js5WorkerThread literal

relationship_to_previous_milestones.provider-descriptor-setup-function-liter: Uses the Js5WorkerThread literal-labeled cluster as the target.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.js5-bootstrap-constructor: Adds a worker-thread/provider bridge candidate to follow back toward bootstrap ownership.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-provider-address-ref-func: Reuses the same provider descriptor and address-reference workflow, now with native .pdata neighborhood lookup.

next_targets: Trace 0x14066DC10, the helper called with Js5WorkerThread in RDX, to identify the worker-name registry or thread launcher.
Trace 0x14001C520-0x14001CDD1, which names client, Js5WorkerThread, MixerWorker, and VorbisWorker in one bootstrap routine.
Trace 0x14059E45B around the 0x14059EC18 materializer call and 0x14059D7B0 dispatch to understand descriptor 0x140B99268's lifecycle.
Build a native descriptor-slot cross-reference summarizer so these materializer/vtable relationships no longer require manual qword-readback stitching.
